is-JointStateInputHandler.description: To indicate that this describes a joint state inputhandler which reads from a sensor_msgs/JointState Message topic and converts it into an array of joint position etasl expression. Only joint positions are taken into account.

topic-name.description: name of the topic to subscribe to

number_of_tries.description: the number of times unsuccesfully initialize() can be called before throwing an error

depth.description: Depth of the topic queue (i.e. buffer size storing incoming messages). Check ROS2 QoS policies for more information

number_of_joints.description: Number of joints for which to create input channels.

default_joint_states.title: default_joint_states
default_joint_states.description: Default joint states (in the specified order) used in case that the topic defined in topic-name is not yet publishing. This field is required if the when_unpublished field is set to use_default.

when_unpublished.description: Defines the behavior of the node when nothing has been published to the topic defined in topic-name during activation time of the node

oneOf.title: Use default value
oneOf.description: Uses the defined default value in the default_joint_states field while nothing has been published to the subscribed topic defined in topic-name. An INFO message will be logged.

oneOf.title: Throw error
oneOf.description: Throws an error if nothing has been published to the subscribed topic defined in topic-name at the time of activation of the node, preventing its activation.

varname_prefix.description: Prefix of the expression variable that was created in the task specification with createInputChannel. The data coming from the topic will be available in the task specification through variables with this prefix, starting with 1 index (first joint). For example, if varname_prefix is set to 'joint', the data of the first joint will be available in the task specification through  createInputChannel('joint_1'), the second joint through createInputChannel('joint_2'), etc.
